src/flit/foundation/focus

      • add(m: FocusManager; node: FocusNode)
        focus
      • focus(m: FocusManager; node: FocusNode)
        focusManager
      • focusManager(): FocusManager
        handleComposingEvent
      • handleComposingEvent(m: FocusManager; composing: string; cursorPos: int)
        handleKeyEvent
      • handleKeyEvent(m: FocusManager; ev: KeyEvent): bool
        newFocusNode
      • newFocusNode(onKey: KeyHandler = nil; onText: TextHandler = nil; onShortcut: ShortcutHandler = nil; onFocusChange: proc (focused: bool) = nil): FocusNode
        next
      • next(m: FocusManager)
        prev
      • prev(m: FocusManager)
        remove
      • remove(m: FocusManager; node: FocusNode)
        unfocus
      • unfocus(m: FocusManager)

Keyboard focus management. The FocusManager owns the currently- focused FocusNode; key events dispatched via the binding route to that node's handler. Widgets that need keyboard input (TextField, custom interactive widgets) own a FocusNode and register it with the manager when they want focus.

Tab cycles focus through registered nodes in registration order. Shift+Tab cycles backwards. The manager handles this automatically as long as the runner forwards Tab key events through handleKeyEvent.

This is intentionally simpler than Flutter's full FocusScope tree. A single flat list of focusables is enough for ~99% of UI work and avoids the complexity of nested focus scopes for now.

Imports

binding

Types

ComposingHandler = proc (node: FocusNode; composing: string; cursorPos: int) {.
    closure.}
Called during IME composition. composing is the in-progress text (e.g. partial CJK input); cursorPos is the byte offset within composing where the IME cursor is. Empty string means composition ended. 
FocusKey = enum
  fkUnknown, fkBackspace, fkDelete, fkEnter, fkEscape, fkTab, fkLeft, fkRight,
  fkUp, fkDown, fkHome, fkEnd, fkPageUp, fkPageDown
Logical key codes the focus system understands. The platform runner translates raw scancodes into these before calling handleKeyEvent. SDL's scancode and keysym values are kept in sync via the runner. 
FocusManager = ref object
  nodes*: seq[FocusNode]
  current*: FocusNode
App-wide focus state. One per binding, accessed via binding.focusManager. The manager owns the focus traversal order (nodes) and the currently-focused node (current). 
FocusNode = ref object
  onKey*: KeyHandler
  onText*: TextHandler
  onShortcut*: ShortcutHandler
  onComposing*: ComposingHandler
  onFocusChange*: proc (focused: bool) {.closure.}
  hasFocus*: bool
  enabled*: bool

A focusable target. The owner widget creates one in initState, registers it via manager.add, and supplies onKey, onText, and (optionally) onShortcut callbacks to receive input.

hasFocus is true while this node is the manager's current. onFocusChange fires whenever that transitions.

KeyHandler = proc (node: FocusNode; key: FocusKey; modifiers: uint32) {.closure.}
ShortcutHandler = proc (node: FocusNode; keysym: int; modifiers: uint32): bool {.
    closure.}
Returns true if the shortcut was consumed. 
TextHandler = proc (node: FocusNode; text: string) {.closure.}

Vars

globalFocus: FocusManager

Procs

proc add(m: FocusManager; node: FocusNode) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Registers node with the manager. Idempotent. Adds at the end of the traversal order. 
proc focus(m: FocusManager; node: FocusNode) {....raises: [Exception, Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Transfers focus to node. The previously focused node, if any, gets hasFocus = false and its onFocusChange(false) fires. 
proc focusManager(): FocusManager {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the app-wide focus manager, creating it on first access. Safe to call from anywhere. 
proc handleComposingEvent(m: FocusManager; composing: string; cursorPos: int) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Routes an IME composition update to the focused node's onComposing callback. Empty string ends composition. 
proc handleKeyEvent(m: FocusManager; ev: KeyEvent): bool {.
    ...raises: [Exception, Exception, Exception, Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
    forbids: [].}

Routes ev to the focused node. Returns true if the event was consumed by focus traversal or by the focused node. Returns false if no node was focused or the event should propagate elsewhere.

Tab and Shift+Tab are intercepted here for traversal. Backspace, Delete, Enter, Escape, arrows, Home, End, PgUp, PgDown are forwarded as FocusKey codes to onKey. Any other key that has a text field gets forwarded to onText.

proc newFocusNode(onKey: KeyHandler = nil; onText: TextHandler = nil;
                  onShortcut: ShortcutHandler = nil;
                  onFocusChange: proc (focused: bool) = nil): FocusNode {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Constructs a fresh focus node. Set callbacks for the events you care about; nil callbacks are ignored. 
proc next(m: FocusManager) {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                             forbids: [].}
Moves focus to the next enabled node in registration order. Wraps around. No-op if no focusables are registered. 
proc prev(m: FocusManager) {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                             forbids: [].}
Reverse of next. 
proc remove(m: FocusManager; node: FocusNode) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Removes node from the manager. Clears focus if the removed node was focused. 
proc unfocus(m: FocusManager) {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                                forbids: [].}
Drops focus from whatever currently has it. Useful when the user clicks outside any focusable widget.
